beats.text: The workbench is covered in half-finished projects. A birdhouse from three Christmases ago. A broken sprinkler head. A red Craftsman toolbox missing half its sockets.

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: Where did I put that 9/16... let me dig around.
You push aside the paint cans, open the toolbox, dig under the tray liner — something's back there.

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: ...what is this.
A small ziplock. A glass piece shaped like a tiny mountain. A lighter with a cartoon frog on it.

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: Oh. OH. That's... huh.
College You left a present for Suburban Dad You. College You always had your back.

choices.text: Take a hit right now (5 min)

beats.text: *click* *bubble bubble bubble* *cough*

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: OH that's — *cough cough* — that's still good.
A deep calm descends. The garage is suddenly interesting. The pegboard holds SO many shapes.

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: I forgot how much I liked the garage.
The minivan hums. Probably. It's parked. It can't hum. But it is humming.

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: Not now. Later. Definitely later.
You slide the ziplock back under the tray liner. It waits patiently. It has all the time in the world.

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: The workbench. And... the OTHER thing on the workbench.
The bong sits on the workbench like a proud little green trophy. The lighter is right there.

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: One more. For the BBQ. For the VIBE.
*bubble bubble* *hold* *HACK*

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: I can HEAR the pegboard.
Karen's car pulls into the driveway in your head, ominously, even though she's at the store. Paranoia is a side effect.

close.text: You step back from the workbench. A socket falls behind the bench. You will never find it.
